title: Cordiera pilosa

intro: Cordiera pilosa is a woody vine native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cordiera pilosa include alkaloids, such as cordioline and cordifoline, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
